annotation_target.structure: Second phrase — LH reaches lower register, harmonic tension increases
key_moments: m9 bass enters new harmonic area
m11 dissonant chord — new chromatic pitch
teaching_goals: feel the darkening of harmonic color
RH chords become slightly fuller here
style_tips: a slight hairpin swell is appropriate in m.11

teaching_notes.note: The dissonance here is deliberate — let it speak without hurrying past.

target_trace.objective: Read mm. 9–12 of Prelude in E Minor, Op. 28 No. 4, view the piano roll, analyze the phrase, play in a loop, and predict the continuation.

session.content: Show me measures 9–12 and describe how the harmony darkens.
Let me view the piano roll for mm. 9–12.

Measures 9–12 deepen the harmonic descent — the LH reaches lower, more dissonant territory. The RH chords grow slightly thicker. There is a sense of inevitability to the downward motion. Let me loop mm. 9–12.
Chopin Prelude E minor mm. 9–12: deepened harmonic descent, growing thickness. Darkening mood.
